title: Initial Self-Assesment Test1
text: Please dedicate the next 15 minutes to the following self assesment text.

title: Initial Self-Assesment Test1

Footnotes

title: The C++ Standard Template Library1
text:
The C++ Standard Template Library (STL) is a collection of templates for several containers (vector, pair, stack, queue, set, etc) that there is no need to re-implement. In this page we will give a short description of some of the templates and methods. For more information see cppreference.com.
To use the STL containers and methods when compiling with `g++ it is necessary to use the flag -std=c++11.
The rationale behind the STL is to have an interface as uniform as possible. For instance, it is possible to give initialization lists to STL containers.
In the same spirit of uniformity, there are several common methods. For instance the followings.
Moreover, since the C++11 standard, if the compiler can infer the type of a variable, instead of writing explicitly its type, it is possible to use auto.
Also for loops got an alternative (less error prone) syntax when used in collections such as set<T>.

title: The C++ Standard Template Library1
The C++ Standard Template Library1

text:
Footnotes


The content of this page is mostly based on STL and Algorithms in C++.↩︎
That is a<b is true when either a.first < b.first or when a.first == b.first and a.second < b.second.↩︎
Internally, they are implemented with balanced binary search trees↩︎
Internally map<K,V> is implemented with balanced binary search trees.↩︎
Internally they are implemented with Hash tables.↩︎
Internally they are implemented with hash tables.↩︎

title: L5: Exhaustive algorithms I
text:
Step 1: Getting everything in place





Choose a problem from Jutge.org to solve.






Common error(s)



The html statement of the problem might not be rendered properly.
The official statement of the problem is in the .pdf file.


Download the .zip archive of the problem and unzip it.
In the obtained folder there are:

a file .pdf with the official problem statement
one or more text files with examples of input sample.inp
the corresponding correct outputs sample.cor










Step 2: Getting the C++ code to compile





After creating a C++ source file to solve the problem, compile it with g++ -std=c++11.






Common error(s)



Not using the flag -std=c++11.


Possible useful flags are -Wall -Wextra -Wno-overflow -Wpedantic -Werror -D_GLIBCXX_DEBUG









Step 3: Testing testing testing





Test the executable file obtained on the given example(s), for instance with ./a.out < sample.inp > output.






Common error(s)



Writing the input by hand.
Not checking the exact formatting of the output.


To see whether the output is the same as sample.cor a way is to use diff output sample.cor









Step 4: Submitting the solution to the Jutge





Once the code compiles and on the given samples it gives the correct output submit it to the Jutge for automatic evaluation.






Common error(s)



Sending to the Jutge code that was not even compiled locally.
Not checking if the output was correct on the given inputs.

title: L6: Exhaustive algorithms II
Data Structures and Algorithmics
text:
Welcome to Data Structures and Algorithmics (EDA)!
I’m Ilario Bonacina and if you are a student of the groups L13 and L43 then I’m one of your instructors for this semester.  The person in charge of the whole course is Albert Oliveras Llunell. For administrative questions please ask him directly. For instance by email.
Please refer to the main webpage of EDA for news, course material, etc and to the official UPC page for the course syllabus.

title: Data Structures and Algorithmics
text:
Contacts
Student hours Wednesdays, 12:00 - 13:00 room Omega 223 
ilario.bonacina+EDA@upc.edu  Please write an email before dopping by my office.  The last possible day to ask questions by email is Wednesday May 29, 2024.

title: Data Structures and Algorithmics
text:
Dynamics of lab classes
Every lab class follows the same structure. In every session there will be some set of exercises from Jutge.org to solve using the programming language C++.

title: Data Structures and Algorithmics
section: Schedule for the groups L13 and L43
text:
Schedule for the groups L13 and L43
The meeting times are the following:
group L13Wednesdays 8:00 – 10:00 room C6 S308 group L43Wednesdays 18:00 – 20:00room C6 S303

title: Data Structures and Algorithmics
text:
References


cppreference.com

and its offline version C++ reference. This last version is available to consult during the lab exam, therefore it is convenient to look at it to see what is not needed to memorize.



EDA Main Page\(\rightarrow\) Material Docent


Algorismes en C++
Normes de Programació (in catalan) This file describes the accepted style of C++ writing.  It is used as a reference for correcting the lab exam.
Eines de Suport a la Programació (in catalan) A list of possibly useful software (source code editors, debuggers, git, …)

Bjarne Stroustrup. Addison-Wesley, (4th ed.) 2013
